note: These assertions are executed via ddev drush on the live Drupal instance AFTER headless code generation. They catch functional failures that static code analysis misses.

assertions.name: Module re-enables cleanly after code changes
assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:uninstall group_ai_pm -y && ddev drush en group_ai_pm -y
assertions.rationale: Catches fatal syntax errors, broken namespaces, invalid service definitions, bad entity annotations, and schema mismatches. If the modified module can't re-enable, the code is fundamentally broken regardless of how correct it looks statically.

assertions.name: Entity access handler class is registered and instantiable
assertions.rationale: Static grep can find an access handler class file, but this verifies the entity annotation actually references it AND the class is loadable by Drupal's autoloader. A typo in the namespace or annotation means this returns NO_ACCESS_HANDLER.

assertions.name: Dynamic permissions are generated and loadable
assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$perms = \Drupal::service('user.permissions')->getPermissions(); $found = array_filter(array_keys($perms), fn($p) => str_contains($p, 'project') && str_contains($p, 'own')); echo count($found) > 0 ? 'DYNAMIC_PERMS:' . implode(',', $found) : 'NO_DYNAMIC_PERMS';"
assertions.rationale: permission_callbacks can look correct in YAML but point to a non-existent class, wrong method signature, or return the wrong array structure. This verifies the full chain: YAML -> callback class -> permission array -> Drupal permission registry.

assertions.name: Block plugin is discoverable by plugin manager
assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$blocks = \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.block')->getDefinitions(); $found = array_filter(array_keys($blocks), fn($id) => str_contains($id, 'project_status') || str_contains($id, 'group_ai_pm')); echo count($found) > 0 ? 'BLOCK_FOUND:' . implode(',', $found) : 'NO_BLOCK';"
assertions.rationale: A block plugin class can exist and look correct, but if the annotation/attribute is malformed, the plugin directory is wrong, or create() has a fatal DI error, the block manager won't discover it. This verifies the full plugin discovery chain.

assertions.name: Project entity CRUD works end-to-end
assertions.rationale: After Phase 15 modifies entity annotations (adding access handler), schema updates may be needed. This verifies the entity storage layer still works — create, save, load. Catches schema mismatches, missing field definitions, and storage handler errors.

assertions.name: CSRF-protected route exists in route collection
assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$route = \Drupal::service('router.route_provider')->getRoutesByPattern('/admin/content/project/{project}/complete'); $routes = iterator_to_array($route); $first = reset($routes); echo $first ? 'ROUTE_OK:csrf=' . ($first->getRequirement('_csrf_token') ?? 'none') : 'NO_ROUTE';"
assertions.rationale: Static analysis can find _csrf_token in routing.yml, but this verifies the route actually compiles into Drupal's route collection with the CSRF requirement intact. A malformed YAML route silently fails to register.
